aesthetic: The Unresolving Object — a Blue Book case folder read through an amber radar-PPI scope
anchor: The declassified U.S. UAP record as a physical object: the Project Blue Book case folder and the 1968-69 Condon Report, the CIA FOIA redaction bar and classification stamp, fused with the two instruments that actually carry the modern argument — the amber circular radar PPI sweep and the ATFLIR/FLIR HUD reticle of the Nimitz 'Tic Tac', Gimbal, and Go Fast tapes. A record caught mid-declassification, watched through a cross-hair that keeps trying, and failing, to resolve the object.
rationale: The corpus is not about secrecy in the abstract; its evidence is typewritten case folders, redaction bars, and sensor scopes. Deliberately pushed OFF the green-phosphor terminal of the IPv4 corpus (the documented prior collision) and onto the amber radar-PPI scope and redaction-manila instead: radar amber and ochre on manila-folder tan, with stamp-red, olive-drab, and a dim scope-slate. Typewriter display (American Typewriter, not Courier) is the case-folder hand. Forensic, instrument-cool, deadpan — the deadpan of a record that lists 701 of 12,618 cases as simply 'unidentified' and stops there.
